summary_line: Field registration covered 90.36% of frames, but player and ball tracking are fragmented: 5,998 player track IDs over 70,025 frames, average player detections 9.233/frame, and average ball detections 0.5583/frame.

diagnostics.title: Field mapping mostly present
diagnostics.message: Field registration succeeded on 58,4240 projected player points with a 0.9036 registered-frame ratio, and the last good calibration reached frame 69,960 out of 70,025. Coverage stayed active through nearly the full clip.
diagnostics.next_step: Use projected coordinates for downstream review, but inspect the final 100 frames to confirm calibration did not drop at the end.

diagnostics.title: Calibration refresh is inconsistent
diagnostics.message: Only 3,622 of 7,003 calibration refresh attempts succeeded, a 0.5172 success rate. Visible pitch keypoints averaged 3.6686, which is sparse for stable refreshes and likely explains the 48% failure rate.
diagnostics.next_step: Review frames around failed refresh intervals and increase visible line coverage in the source crop or camera selection before rerunning calibration.

diagnostics.title: Player tracks are highly fragmented
diagnostics.message: The run produced 5,998 unique player track IDs from 646,539 player rows, with average track length 107.4803 frames and churn ratio 0.085655. Longest track length was 1,242 frames, so a few long tracks exist, but most identities are short-lived relative to the 70,025-frame clip.
diagnostics.next_step: Spot-check identity continuity in crowded sequences and retune tracker association settings before using per-player longitudinal stats.

diagnostics.title: Ball coverage is thin
diagnostics.message: Ball detections averaged 0.5583 per frame, with 39,093 ball rows across 70,025 frames and 1,388 unique ball track IDs. That indicates the ball is missing in a large share of frames and ball identity is frequently reset.
diagnostics.next_step: Audit ball detection on fast transitions and aerial phases, then rerun with ball-specific threshold or tracking adjustments if ball events matter for this analysis.

diagnostics.title: Goal experiment is not discriminative here
diagnostics.message: The goal-aligned experiment is present, but with only 1 goal in the clip its signal is weak. The 30s pre-goal volatility index was 0.1328 versus a baseline of -0.0183, yet reported 30s uplift is 0.0, so this run does not show a clear goal-separation result.
diagnostics.next_step: Do not use this experiment alone for goal-state decisions on this clip; compare against more goal-containing runs before acting on it.
